content.full_text:
A-81-196

April 15, 1981

Louis Dexter
Human Resource Development
305 No. Ranchleo Street
Anaheim, California 92801

Dear Mr. Dexter:

A Recipient Committee is not required to file a Statement of
Organization prior to the receipt of $500 (Government Code Section
8610(b)). However, a Recipient Committee may file a Statement of
Organization before receiving $500 if it so chooses.

If I can be of further assistance, please feel free to contact me.

Sincerely,

Lynn Montgomery
Political Reform Consultant

LOUIS DEXTER
HUMAN RESOURCE DEVELOPMENT
305 NORTH RANCHITO STREET
ANAHEIM, CALIF. 92801

4/3/01

Sir—

Please send me the latest copy of the
Amended Fair Political Practices Act as
offered in your latest FPAC newsletter.

Also, please answer the following
question: Can a "Recipient Committee"
be formed although the authorized
contribution may free short of the
$500 requirement? In other words,
I would like to form a political
activity committee but am not sure
at this stage about response. I
have intentions of filing statement
of organization with Secretary of State
if I may do so. Louis Dexter

sections.question: CAN A "RECIPIENT COMMITTEE" BE FORMED ALTHOUGH THE ANTICIPATED CONTRIBUTIONS MAY FALL SHORT OF THE $500 REQUIREMENT? IN OTHER WORDS, I WOULD LIKE TO FORM A POLITICAL ACTIVITY COMMITTEE BUT AM NOT SURE AT THIS TIME ABOUT RESPONSE. I HAVE INTENTIONS OF FILING STATEMENT OF ORGANIZATION WITH SECRETARY OF STATE IF I MAY DO SO.
sections.conclusion: A Recipient Committee is not required to file a Statement of Organization prior to the receipt of $500 (Government Code Section 84101). However, a Recipient Committee may file a Statement of Organization before receiving $500 if it so chooses.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a Recipient Committee can be formed and file a Statement of Organization with the Secretary of State when anticipated contributions may fall short of the $500 requirement.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: A Recipient Committee is not required to file a Statement of Organization until it receives $500, but it may voluntarily file one before reaching that threshold if it chooses to do so.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains incoming request followed by FPPC response letter. Both sections present in single document. Clear question and conclusion sections identified from the correspondence.

QUESTION: CAN A "RECIPIENT COMMITTEE" BE FORMED ALTHOUGH THE ANTICIPATED CONTRIBUTIONS MAY FALL SHORT OF THE $500 REQUIREMENT? IN OTHER WORDS, I WOULD LIKE TO FORM A POLITICAL ACTIVITY COMMITTEE BUT AM NOT SURE AT THIS TIME ABOUT RESPONSE. I HAVE INTENTIONS OF FILING STATEMENT OF ORGANIZATION WITH SECRETARY OF STATE IF I MAY DO SO.

CONCLUSION: A Recipient Committee is not required to file a Statement of Organization prior to the receipt of $500 (Government Code Section 84101). However, a Recipient Committee may file a Statement of Organization before receiving $500 if it so chooses.

embedding.summary: FPPC advises that while Recipient Committees are not required to file a Statement of Organization until receiving $500 in contributions, they may opt to file voluntarily before reaching that threshold.
